channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-21
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. In the `scienceverse/metacheck` repository, Lakens made notable contributions by fixing a crash in the code check and enhancing the functionality for loading paper databases from `scienceverse/papers`. Meanwhile, the `giladfeldman/escicheck` project was updated to sync with the latest versions of `effectcheck`, specifically versions `0.6.3` and `0.6.4`. Additionally, the `shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve` and `giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward` projects saw revisions to their validation note and the addition of a Chrome Webstore link, respectively. These updates demonstrate the connections and collaborations within our community, and we celebrate the progress made towards improving our open-source research tools. Overall, these changes highlight the community's commitment to enhancing the stability, functionality, and accessibility of our projects.

122 commits across 7 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

regcheck37 commits
docpluck31 commits
coarse30 commits
metacheck20 commits
escicheck2 commits

item.title: docpluck: 5 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, where a feature was added to flatten fields for non-clinical result tables as part of `REQUEST_11` in version `v2.4.95`. The documentation was also updated to log the shipment of `v2.4.95` and `REQUEST_11`, as well as to note deferred follow-ups including "effect_type opt-in, 77859 caption-number, text-layer caveat". Additionally, the documentation was updated to record the cross-session skill-file commit coordination, which is now fulfilled, and to reflect prior-session housekeeping including the `REQUEST_10` customer broadcast and deploy smoke-gate fix. Furthermore, the library and application version-pin sync was enforced through a directive and QA/review/deploy gate.

5 commit(s) today:

  • 2dbdd98 [main] docs+tooling: enforce library↔app version-pin sync (directive + qa/review/deploy gate) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was updated with a sync from `escicheckapp` at commit `dec37b1`, which corresponds to `effectcheck` version `0.6.4`, as stated in the commit message "sync from escicheckapp@dec37b1 (effectcheck v0.6.4)". Prior to this, the `main` branch was also updated with a sync from `escicheckapp` at commit `f52d7ce`, which corresponds to `effectcheck` version `0.6.3`, as per the commit message "sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3)". No other branches were worked on, with all changes occurring on the `main` branch. These commits brought the `main` branch up to date with the latest versions of `effectcheck`, specifically versions `0.6.3` and `0.6.4`. The specific changes made in these versions of `effectcheck` are not detailed in the commit messages, but they are now reflected in the `main` branch.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 1d48c4e [main] sync from escicheckapp@dec37b1 (effectcheck v0.6.4) (#18) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were worked on according to the provided commit messages. The specific change made was the addition of a link, with no other features or fixes mentioned. There were no changes to existing modules or addition of new ones, aside from the added link. The commit message explicitly mentions the addition of the Chrome Webstore link, indicating this was the sole purpose of the commit.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
